name: E3NN I_c Local Unitary Invariance

pytorch.reason: density matrix ops, partial trace, entropy, SU(2) unitaries

z3.reason: supportive: encode I_c invariance as symbolic constraints

cvc5.reason: supportive: cross-check local unitary invariance proof

e3nn.reason: PRIMARY: generate SU(2) rotations via wigner_D; FCTP equivariance pipeline

P1_ic_invariant_local_unitaries_both_sid.test: I_c(U_A ⊗ U_B rho U_A† ⊗ U_B†) = I_c(rho) for 50 random SU(2) pairs × 5 states
P1_ic_invariant_local_unitaries_both_sid.note: I_c = S(B) - S(AB). Both S(B) and S(AB) are unitarily invariant. U_A changes only S(A), not S(B) or S(AB). U_B changes S(B) → but S(rho_B) is invariant under U_B since eigenvalues of U_B rho_B U_B† = eigs(rho_B). S(AB) invariant under any local unitary. Tolerance 1e-6 reflects eigenvalue computation noise for near-singular matrices (Bell state has zero eigenvalue).

P2_ic_invariant_ua_only.test: I_c(U_A ⊗ I rho U_A† ⊗ I) = I_c(rho) for 50 random U_A × 5 states
P2_ic_invariant_ua_only.note: I_c = S(B) - S(AB). U_A ⊗ I leaves rho_B unchanged (tracing A gives same rho_B). S(AB) invariant since eigs of (U_A ⊗ I) rho (U_A ⊗ I)† = eigs of rho. Tolerance 1e-6 reflects eigvalsh precision for near-singular Bell states.

P3_ic_invariant_ub_only.test: I_c(I ⊗ U_B rho I ⊗ U_B†) = I_c(rho) for 50 random U_B × 5 states
P3_ic_invariant_ub_only.note: S(B) invariant under U_B since eigs preserved. S(AB) invariant under I ⊗ U_B.

P4_SB_and_SAB_individually_invariant.test: S(B) and S(AB) individually invariant under local unitaries (Bell state, 50 trials)
P4_SB_and_SAB_individually_invariant.note: Invariance of S(B) and S(AB) independently confirms I_c invariance mechanistically. Tolerance 1e-6 reflects eigvalsh precision on near-singular Bell state.

N1_ic_not_invariant_joint_unitaries.test: I_c changes under arbitrary joint unitary V on AB (Werner state, 20 random V)
N1_ic_not_invariant_joint_unitaries.note: Arbitrary joint unitary can change entanglement structure and hence I_c. This is expected: I_c is an entanglement measure, not a joint-unitary invariant.

N2_nn_linear_fails_equivariance.test: nn.Linear I_c predictor is NOT equivariant under SO(3) rotation of Bloch vectors
N2_nn_linear_fails_equivariance.note: Non-equivariant model cannot reliably predict invariant quantities under rotation.

N3_product_state_ic_zero.note: For |00><00|: S(B)=0 (pure state), S(AB)=0 (pure state) → I_c=0. Invariance under local unitaries trivially holds (0=0).

B1_identity_local_unitary_zero_deviation.test: I ⊗ I (identity) gives zero I_c deviation

B2_canonical_ic_values.test: I_c values for canonical states match theory
B2_canonical_ic_values.note: Bell: S(B)=log(2), S(AB)=0; Product: S(B)=S(AB)=0; MaxMix: S(B)=log(2), S(AB)=2*log(2)

B3_extreme_su2_rotations_bell_state.test: I_c invariance under Pauli/Hadamard local gates on Bell state
B3_extreme_su2_rotations_bell_state.note: Pauli gates are SU(2) elements (up to phase); I_c must be invariant.

B4_wigner_D_rotation_matrix_check.test: e3nn wigner_D(1) is a proper rotation matrix (det=1, orthogonal)
B4_wigner_D_rotation_matrix_check.note: Confirms e3nn rotation generators are valid for the invariance tests.

Z3_1_ic_invariant_iff_both_entropies_inv.test: UNSAT: impossible for I_c to change if S(B) and S(AB) are individually invariant
Z3_1_ic_invariant_iff_both_entropies_inv.note: If S_B_new = S_B and S_AB_new = S_AB, then I_c_new = S_B_new - S_AB_new = I_c. The assumption I_c_new ≠ I_c is contradictory. z3 finds UNSAT.

Z3_2_unitary_conjugation_preserves_eigen.test: UNSAT: unitary conjugation cannot change eigenvalues of density matrix
Z3_2_unitary_conjugation_preserves_eigen.note: Tr(UMU†) = Tr(M) and det(UMU†) = det(M) → same characteristic polynomial → same eigenvalues. Z3 finds it UNSAT to have different eigenvalues under this constraint.

Z3_3_global_unitary_can_change_ic_SAT.test: SAT: global unitary can change S(B) while S(AB) fixed, thus changing I_c
Z3_3_global_unitary_can_change_ic_SAT.note: Under V on AB: S(AB) invariant (global trace), but rho_B = Tr_A[V rho V†] changes. So S(B) and hence I_c can change. This contrasts with local unitary invariance.

CVC5_ic_invariant_local_unitaries.test: CVC5 UNSAT: I_c cannot change if S(B) and S(AB) individually invariant
CVC5_ic_invariant_local_unitaries.note: Algebraic cross-check of z3 result; confirms invariance is logically necessary.

e3nn_fctp_pipeline.test: e3nn FCTP (1o,1o)→0e trained to predict I_c; test equivariance under SO(3)

simultaneous_rotation_equivariance.note: 0e output from FCTP is invariant under SO(3) by construction

asymmetric_rotation_prediction_stability.note: I_c is invariant under one-sided rotations too, so predictions should be stable under asymmetric rotation if FCTP has learned the true invariant.

key_finding: I_c = S(B) - S(AB) is invariant under local unitaries U_A ⊗ U_B. This holds for both-sided AND one-sided local unitaries (stronger claim). Mechanism: eigenvalues of rho_B preserved under U_B; eigenvalues of rho_AB preserved under U_A⊗U_B. I_c is NOT invariant under arbitrary joint unitaries (confirmed numerically). z3 UNSAT confirms logical necessity of invariance. cvc5 cross-checks z3. e3nn FCTP (1o,1o)→0e is equivariant by construction.
